prompt:
A chemistry planning card laid out on a bench, showing a stepwise titration plan through drop-count columns, mixing arrows, expected pH windows, and branching adjustment logic implied by diagrams instead of readable text. Small droppers, a beaker, and a pH reference setup nearby make it obvious this sheet is a pre-run plan for dropwise acid or base additions to hit a target pH range. The page should feel calculated and procedural rather than casual notes.

Ultra-detailed, photorealistic 512×512 image with a clean near-future lab aesthetic: soft overhead task lighting, realistic paper, glass, and plastic textures, no logos, no readable text, and no identifiable faces. Keep the planning card central and uncluttered so it reads clearly as a titration strategy artifact.
